input.prompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

data.content: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:30:30.872Z</current_datetime>

A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

input.initialPrompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Check binlog file type and size

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check binlog file type and size

arguments.command: which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null || echo "dotnet not found"

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build binary log (binlog) file to diagnose build failures. Let me start by examining the binlog file and extracting useful information from it.

Binary log files (.binlog) are MSBuild structured log files. I can use the `dotnet build` tool or the MSBuild Structured Log Viewer CLI to analyze them. Let me first check what tools are available and then try to extract error information from the binlog.

Let me try a few approaches:
1. Check if `dotnet` is available
2. Try using `dotnet msbuild` to replay the binlog
3. Or use the structured log viewer CLI tool


4. First, I'll verify the binlog file exists and check its basic properties

arguments.description: Check binlog file type and size

arguments.command: which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null || echo "dotnet not found"

toolCalls.args: {"command": "file /tmp/sv-8ac5358460c449978f825cc1188f68db/build.binlog && ls -lh /tmp/sv-8ac5358460c449978f825cc1188f68db/build.binlog", "description": "Check binlog file type and size", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"dotnet not found\"", "description": "Check if dotnet is available", "initial_wait": 10}

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Try replaying binlog with dotnet build

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try replaying binlog with dotnet build

arguments.description: Try replaying binlog with dotnet build

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8ac5358460c449978f825cc1188f68db && dotnet build build.binlog 2>&1 | head -50", "description": "Try replaying binlog with dotnet build", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8ac5358460c449978f825cc1188f68db && dotnet msbuild -flp:logfile=build.log -nologo build.binlog 2>&1 | head -50", "description": "Try msbuild replay of binlog", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Try replaying binlog with dotnet build
